summary: Obfuscated import-time dropper fetches and executes remote payload
details: subsearch@1.0.3 ships a single obfuscated index.js (RC4-encrypted 231-entry string array with rotating keys, hex-numeric literal constants, control-flow flattening) as its main entry. On require(), the code reconstructs a hardcoded IPv4 address via four ''.concat(octet,'.') calls, performs an HTTP GET to a URL built from that IP, writes the response body to a file under os.tmpdir(), and immediately invokes child_process on the dropped file with cwd set to tmpdir. Any project that requires this package executes attacker-controlled bytes fetched from the hardcoded endpoint. Package metadata reinforces the dropper shape: empty description, empty author, no repository/homepage/keywords, ISC boilerplate, single-file body, generic English name. Heavy obfuscation conceals the destination IP and the dropped command from casual review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
